Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (aggravating circumstance √¢¬Ä¬î evacuation order or emergency)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Criminal Code (aggravating circumstance √¢¬Ä¬î evacuation order or emergency)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Criminal Code to provide that taking advantage of the fact that a place is under an evacuation order or is experiencing a natural disaster or emergency is to be considered an aggravating circumstance for sentencing purposes.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Paragraph 718.√¢¬Ä¬ç2(a) of the Criminal Code is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (iii.√¢¬Ä¬ç2):

Subparagraph.Label: (iii.3)
Subparagraph.Text: evidence that the offender, in committing the offence, took advantage of the fact that the place where the offence was committed was under a lawful evacuation order or was experiencing a natural disaster or other emergency,

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 1:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portion of section 718.2:

Section.Text: A court that imposes a sentence shall also take into consideration the following principles:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a sentence should be increased or reduced to account for any relevant aggravating or mitigating circumstances relating to the offence or the offender, and, without limiting the generality of the foregoing,
